articles.title: Telstra executive says server software update could have prevented outage - video
articles.description: Telstra's executive for end-to-end resilience, Gerard Tracey, concedes that had the company invested in replacing a 15-year-old server, which cost $30,000, an outage last Wednesday that cut hundreds of people from triple zero and disrupted services nationally may have been prevented. In a senate inquiry into triple zero, Tracey says: 'A newer piece of hardware operating in the same design that we intended to, the issue wouldn’t have happened'. articles.title: Pauline Hanson and Tommy Robinson meeting becomes grist for News Corp’s social media mill | Weekly Beast
articles.description: Video by far-right anti-Islamic activist is republished on Sydney tabloid’s social media accounts. Plus: Qantas has a speedy retort for commentator kept waiting Karl Stefanovic’s decision to interview with Tommy Robinson last month was considered so out of step with mainstream media standards it led to his termination from Nine.
